strengths: Effective use of self-management strategies with concrete examples (timers, alerts, Asana)
Strong information literacy shown by a structured research plan and application of CRAAP criteria
Demonstrated collaborative and communication skills through peer support integration
Transfer of learning to product design via user tutorials, flowcharts, and careful application of design elements like Google Fonts
weaknesses: Limited explanation of how specific ATL skills directly improved the quality or functionality of the website
No concrete follow-up actions outlined to address identified technical gaps (e.g., account data storage)
Insufficient connection between organizational/research efforts and observable product development outcomes
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The student clearly applied a range of ATL skillsâself-management, research, collaborationâand provided relevant examples of each. To strengthen this section, they should draw explicit links between these skills and tangible improvements in the website, and outline targeted next steps for unresolved technical challenges.

strengths: Clear articulation of personal interest linked to past coding and coaching experiences
Convincing, evidence-based connection between the fitness website goal and rugby background
Comprehensive success criteria table covering aesthetics, functionality, personalization, and account features
Detailed Gantt chart with logical sequencing, deadlines, and time allocation
weaknesses: Success criteria are not expressed as measurable milestones for tracking skill development
Narrative action plan omits specific resources (software, tutorials, mentorship) needed to support feasibility
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The planning section demonstrates a strong, evidence-based rationale and a detailed roadmapâespecially through the success criteria table and Gantt chartâproviding clarity and focus. To enhance this plan further, the student should translate success criteria into measurable milestones and explicitly identify the tools, resources, and supports required to achieve each phase of the project.

strengths: Thorough evaluation of the product against each success criterion with specific examples
Insightful reflection on expanding target audiences and addressing inclusivity gaps
Clear articulation of personal growth in both technical coding skills and fitness expertise
Thoughtful recognition of gender inclusivity issues with concrete plans for tailored programming
weaknesses: Customer satisfaction analysis is honest but lacks concrete improvement suggestions to enhance user experience
Reflection on learning impact is descriptive and could better reference original success criteria to demonstrate guided growth
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The reflection is comprehensive, using evidence to evaluate the productâs strengths and identify user needs, and demonstrating deep personal and technical growth. To elevate this reflection, the student should propose specific enhancements based on user feedback and explicitly link learning outcomes back to the initial success criteria.

annotations.comment: You acknowledge limitations in the account data storage feature but donât describe follow-up actions. Outline concrete steps (e.g. specific API or storage methods) to address this gap.

Your customer satisfaction rating is honest but lacks suggestions for enhancement. Propose targeted improvements (e.g. integrating user playlists or embedding interactive calendars) to elevate satisfaction.
You clearly articulate personal interest in coding and digital design, linking past experiences to your learning goal. This rationale strengthens motivation and focus for your project.
Your product goal of a fitness website connects directly to your rugby experience and coaching background, demonstrating a convincing, evidence-based link to personal interest.

You state your learning goal to master HTML, CSS and JavaScript, but your success criteria arenât measurable. Consider defining specific milestones (e.g. complete 10 coding exercises weekly) to track progress effectively.
The success criteria table is detailed and covers aesthetics, functionality, personalization and account features. This breadth provides a solid framework for evaluating your product.
Your Gantt chart provides clear sequencing of tasks, deadlines and time allocation. This detailed timeline enhances feasibility and helps you track progress effectively.
The narrative action plan outlines what you will do and when, but omits specific resources (e.g. software, tutorial sources, mentorship). Add these details to strengthen feasibility.
You describe applying self-management skills with concrete examples (timers, alerts, Asana). This shows effective organization to achieve your learning goal.
Your description of a structured research plan and use of CRAAP criteria demonstrates strong information literacy, ensuring credible sources guide your product development.
You critically reflect on challenges with time management and skipped lessons, showing honest self-evaluation of your self-management ATL skill and its impact on learning progress.

While you detail ATL skills for learning, thereâs limited explanation of how these skills directly improved your product development. Connect specific organizational or research actions to improvements in your websiteâs quality.
Your table systematically evaluates the product against each success criterion, fully supported with specific examples. This thorough approach meets high reflection standards.
You reflect on extending your audience to older demographics and plan to add weight-loss workouts, demonstrating thoughtful evaluation of product impact on diverse users.
Your recognition of gender inclusivity gaps and intention to develop separate programs shows strong reflective thinking on product relevance and equity.
Your reflection on learning impact is descriptive but could be strengthened by explicitly linking outcomes to your original success criteria, showing how each criterion guided your learning.
You highlight collaborative and communication skills gained through peer support, illustrating effective transfer of interpersonal ATL skills to your learning process.
You articulate growth in subject knowledge and fitness expertise, demonstrating how the project deepened both technical and content knowledge.
Creating user tutorials and flowcharts demonstrates effective transfer of learning to product design, showing how your learning process directly informed website development.
Integrating Google Fonts and specifying font-family rules shows careful application of design skills, enhancing readability and consistency across your website.
